1.premise: Pitching Tourette's as a superpower fails when a child exposes its worst flaw and a wrestling move ends the career.
Framing Tourette's as a superpower implies strength, but a child reveals the disqualifying flaw: losing every game of hide and seek.

2.premise: Retaliating against mocking children could be reframed as the only way to lose a motivational speaking career.

1.premise: A Tourette's tic could be reframed as a greeter accidentally insulting every customer after welcoming them.

lines.text: - What's good everybody?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: So my name is Zachariah Tippett.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I have this cool thing called Tourette syndrome.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And tonight, fuck, y'all need to know
lines.label: setup

lines.text: that children are mean.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Fuck, I used to explain Tourette syndrome
lines.label: setup

lines.text: as sort of a superpower.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Fuck, and then one kid stood up and yelled,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: "What makes you think you to be a superhero?"
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Fuck, you can't even win a game of hide and seek.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Fuck.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Fuck. They emote it on me.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Fuck.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Fuck. So after a few triple H moves later.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Fuck.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I'm no longer allowed to be a motivational speaker.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Fuck.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Fuck. Now I'm a Walmart greeter.
lines.label: setup

lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Welcome. Fuck you.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Thank you everybody.
